prompt: Optimize this MySQL query that's running slow because YEAR() wraps the date column...
assertions: Output identifies YEAR(o_orderdate) as non-sargable and explains why it prevents index usage
Rewritten SQL uses a range condition (>= and <) instead of the YEAR() function
Plan comparison is included with before/after metrics (rows examined, access type, key used)
Output explicitly mentions the index that would be used (idx_orders_date or idx_orders_o_orderdate)
Output explains that ORDER BY DESC benefits from backward index scan
